What it means: The amount GROWS each time it is applied, and is permanent.
Examples: Viego (every attack adds another stack, permanently), Kled, Kalista's spears.

What it means: The amount SHRINKS over time until it is gone.
Examples: Sion's bonus HP decays 20/13/0% per second; Irelia's shield decays.

What it means: The amount SHRINKS with each unit it passes through.
Examples: Sona -33% per hit, Jhin -56% per hit. A pierce that is worth less the deeper it goes.

What it means: The amount is MULTIPLIED by a counter the caster is holding.
Examples: Aphelios deals bonus damage per Chakram equipped; Kalista's true damage is per spear removed. The counter itself is an Effect (see 'Note - Stacks').

What it means: The amount is applied REPEATEDLY while the action runs.
Examples: Garen per spin, Swain per second. Pairs with Cadence = Over Time.

What it means: The accumulated amount lands ALL AT ONCE, at the end.
Examples: Swain and Shen. The opposite of Per Tick.

What it means: A bonus that applies ONLY when a condition holds.
Examples: Cassiopeia +30% if Wounded, Sett +50% if only one enemy was grabbed, Ahri +33% if essence stolen. The condition is in the Condition column; the size of the bonus is here.

What it means: The amount SCALES OFF some OTHER quantity — on top of its own per-star Amount.
Examples: Sejuani increases with max Health, Irelia with the damage her shield absorbed, Samira and Aphelios with AP. NOTE: this does not mean 'not a stat'. The per-star Amount already carries the ordinary AD/AP scaling (e.g. '200/300/450% AP'); Derived is for a SECOND quantity layered on top of it. Samira's flat 10/15/20 shred that also grows with AP is exactly that.

What it means: The amount scales with HOW MANY targets were hit.
Examples: Renekton +30% AP per additional enemy, Nasus steals from each enemy hit, Aphelios gains +1 Chakram per enemy hit by the blast.

What it means: An upper BOUND on the effect, not a multiplier.
Examples: Azir may have at most 3 Sand Soldiers on the board.

Scaling Type: Scaling Type says HOW the amount changes; the Scaling column beside it says by how much. Same split as Effect Category / Effect Detail: a vocabulary, then the specifics.

If a cell here would say how LONG something lasts, it belongs in Duration. If it would say how MANY instances fire, it belongs in Count / Spread. If it just restates what the effect is, it belongs nowhere - Effect Types already defines that. Scaling had been holding all three, which is why it needed a vocabulary.
